svg.text: Connection Pool Exhaustion: Failure Timeline and Recovery Playbook
How a traffic spike cascades into system-wide failure and how to prevent it with circuit breakers, queue limits, and proactive monitoring
Pool Exhausted - All Connections In Use
Layered protections that prevent pool exhaustion from cascading into system-wide failure
If queue wait > 500ms → reject with 429 Too Many Requests
Queue depth > 70% → scale up workers | Queue full → shed load
Recovery Playbook: From Failure to Stability
Proactive Guardrails: Pool Sizing and Alerting Thresholds
Pool Size = (Core Count × 2) + Effective Disk Spindles + 20% buffer for burst
Example: 8-core server with SSD = (8 × 2) + 4 + 20% = 24 connections minimum
Alert on: pool utilization, queue depth, wait time, connection leak duration
